sprint: Decoration Image Failures Now Log a Diagnostic Instead of Failing Silently
task: authored a theme in builder this is how it shows up in studio -- a Cover page showed only a flat, unstyled coloured rectangle with a real uploaded "Paper BG" image nowhere to be seen. Investigation traced the visible shape to #previewCanvas's own CSS (rounded corners, 4:5 aspect) painted by the theme's base fillStyle, and confirmed _layerDrawDecorationImage/_ensureDecorationImage silently return/no-op with zero console signal whenever a Decoration image's resolved src ever fails to load/decode -- a broken/expired signed URL, a cross-owner Storage access denial (plausible for a World-Card-redeemed viewer, a different Supabase identity than the Builder author), or any other resolution failure all looked identical: nothing drawn, no error anywhere. Fixed by adding a real, additive-only diagnostic: a console.warn fires when AssetStore.resolve() itself returns null, and another fires on the underlying Image's own onerror -- naming the failed src either way. Zero change to what's drawn for a working image or a genuinely still-loading one. Verified via a new Playwright suite confirming the warning fires correctly for a broken image and never fires for a real one. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
